source_text: DES ILES, Laurence Emanuel.—b. 1891 ; clk., Trin., 1907 ; ch. clk., 1928 ; asst. warden, dist. admin., 1930 ; senr. asst., 1933 ; pensions offr. and ch. inspr. poor relief, 1940.

DICKES, Alan William.—b. 1900; ed. City of London Sch.; on mil. serv. 1941–45, maj.; clk., Br. Sol. Is. Prot., 1925; postmstr., 1927; 1st clk., treas., 1928; asst. treas., G. & E. Is. Prot., 1939; senr. acctnt., acctnt-gen. dept., Nig., 1946; ch. acctnt., colliery dep., 1948.

DONOVAN, Charles Ernest, C.B.E. (1950). —b. 1902; ed. Wesley Coll., Dublin and Dublin Univ., M.A. (Dublin), higher dip. educ. (Dublin) ; inspr., schs., Ken., 1927; ch. inspr., 1939; dep. dir., educ., 1944; dir., educ., S.L., 1946.

DOXAT, Edmund Joseph.—b. 1910; ed. Malvern Coll.; police const., Pal., 1931; inspr., police, B. Hond., 1937; supt., police, G. & E. Is. Col., 1940; asst. supt., G.C., 1942; senr. asst., 1945; supt., 1948.

EGERTON, Frank Pownall, A.M.C.T., M.I.E.E.—b. 1900; ed. King Edward VI Sch., Stafford and Manchester Univ. (Coll. of Techn.); on mil. serv., 1944–45, col.; asst. elec. engr., 1926; exec., 1938; senr. elec. engr., Nig., 1943; Mal. Planning Unit, Lond., and B.M.A., Mal., 1944; dir., elec., Mal., 1946.
FRIEND, Charles Nunan, M.I.Mar.E. (Lond.).—ed. Newcastle Tech. Coll., Australia ; chart. mar. engnr. ; mech. engnr., G. and E.I. Col., 1949.

FRAPPELL, J. O.—b. 1907 ; ed. Edwards Coll., L'pool ; asst. acctnt., G.C. rly., 1931 ; acctnt., 1946 ; staff acctnt., 1947 ; senr. acctnt., 1947 ; chief acctnt. and exec. offr., medical dept., 1948 ; prin. acctnt., G.C. rly., 1949 ; dep. chief acctnt., 1951 ; chief acctnt., 1952 (G.C. local serv.).

FRASER, ROSE DEMPSTER.—Comptroller of customs and navigation laws, and registrar of shipping, Barbados; entered the imperial customs, as landing-waiter and searcher at Bermuda, 1838; appointed senior searcher, 1839; nominated 8th April 1845, by warrant from governor of Bermuda (the late Sir W. Reid), to survey and report on all public moneys in the colonial treasury; nominated acting landing surveyor, 1848; appointed same year landing waiter and searcher at Bridgetown, Barbadoes; appointed landing waiter and searcher at Kingston, Jamaica, 1850; which appointment was cancelled in the year following, at his request, and on nomination as acting landing surveyor, Barbados; appointed first-class landing waiter, colonial customs, Barbados, 1853; landing surveyor, 1855; and comptroller, &c., 1857.

GREGORY-SMITH, Henry Graham.—b. 1899; ed. Rugby and Sandhurst; on mil. serv. 1918, lieut. and 1940–42, maj.; admin. offr., Ken., 1929; comsnr. of the interior, B. Guiana, 1946; mem., settlement comsn., 1947; res. comsnr., B. Sol. Is. Prot., 1950.

HAWKINS, A. CESAR.—Entered the army as ensign in the 1st royals, Feb. 1836, and retired after 12 years service; appointed resident magistrate of the Weenen, in colony of Natal, Sept. 1852, and removed to resident magistracy of the Upper Umcomas division of that colony, Feb. 1855; was a major in a yeomanry cavalry regiment of volunteers called the Natal carbiniers.

HARDY, Donald Peveril.—b. 1912; ed. Middlesbrough High Sch., Victoria Univ., and Selwyn Coll., Camb. (sometime clasfical schol.), M.A. (Cantab.), I.C.S. open comp., 1934; asst. mag., U.P., 1935; joint mag., 1937; collr. and dist. mag., 1940; dep. sec., civ. def. dept., U.P. govt., 1942; reg. food contrlr., 1944; left India, 1947; retd. from I.C.S., 1948; asst. col. sec., G.C., 1948; dist. comsnr. Ashanti, 1949.

HENSHAW, Lawrence Ekening Richard, O.B.E. (hon.) (1948). M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.T.M. (Liv.), L.M. (Coombe, Dub.).—b. 1898; ed. Sacred Heart Sch., Calabar, Nig., King's Coll., Lagos, Edin. Univ. and Liverpool Univ.; junr. med. offr., Nig., 1928; med. offr., 1930; senr., 1949; author of Hepatic Carcinoma and Ovarian Tumour .

HETHERINGTON, Harry Brown, O.B.E., M.D. (Toronto), M.C.P. & S. (Ont.), C.P.H. (Johns Hopkins).—b. 1891 ; ed. Univ. of Toronto, Canada, and Johns Hopkins Univ. ; on mil. serv. 1917–19, capt. ; asst. med. offr., B. Hond., 1920 ; S.M.O., B. Sol. Is. Prot., 1926 ; D.M.S., B. Guiana, 1942.

HOWE, R. L.—b. 1914; ed. Gram. Sch., Teignmouth, and Bristol Univ.; mil. serv., 1939–46; asst. engnr., E.A.P. & T. admin., 1949; exec. engnr., 1952; sen. exec. engnr., 1954; prin. exec. engnr., 1960–62.

KEEN, Arthur William Hoyer, O.B.E. (1950), B.Sc., A.M.I.E.E.—b. 1885; ed. Grammar Sch. and Imp. Coll. of Sci. and Tech., S. Kensington; on war serv., 1918–19, 2nd lt.; asst. engr., N.S.W. Gov., 1913; 2nd lt., R.A.F., 1918–19; asst. engrng. inspr., C.A., 1919; engnrg. inspr., 1919; asst. engr., 1939; dep. ch., 1944; dep. ch. engr. (mech.), 1948.
KNIGHTS, Arthur Ivor James.—b. 1901; ed. Storrington Coll., Sussex; asst. supt., police, Trin., 1921; supt., 1928.

KILLICK, G. L. B.—2nd lieut. N. York rifle militia, May, 1879; 60th King's Roy. Rifles Feb., 1881; Egypt war, 1882 (med. and clp., Tel-el-Kebir and Khedival br. star); Eastern Soudan camp, 1884 (clps., Suakim and El-Teb); temporary capt. army serv. corps, 1887; capt., King's Roy. Rifles, 1890; ret. on gratuity, 1893; capt., 8th vol. batt. K.R.R., 1896; major and comdt., Sarawak Rangers, Apl., 1899.

LEVER, Robert John Aylwin Wallace, B.Sc. (hons.) (Dunelm), D.I.C. (Lond.), A.I.C.T.A., F.L.S.—b. 1905 ; ed. Durham Univ., Imp. Coll. of Sci., Lond., and I.C.T.A. ; entomologist, B. Sol. Is. Prot., 1930 ; Fiji, 1937 ; Mal., 1946.

McCULLOCH, David William, A.Inst.W.E.—b. 1901; asst. engr., Kumasi water supply, G.C., 1924; exec. engr., P.W.D., 1928; maintenance engr., water wks., 1930; exec. engr., gr. I, 1939; senr. pub. health engr., 1943; asst. D.P.W., 1945; D.D.P.W., 1948.

MACFARLANE, Alaric Lois.—b. 1905; ed. Beckford and Smith's Sec. Sch., Spanish Town; junr. clk., P.W.D., J'ca., 1923; clk. and cashier, gr. I, 1938; 1st cl. clk., agric. loan soc. bd., 1940; senr. clk., 1944; examr., accts., 1947; sec. to comtee. on dredging of dry harbour, rlwy. comsn. of enq., 1942, and hurricane relief comtee., 1944.
MAJEKODUNMI, Afolabi Olusola, M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), L.M. (Dub.)—b. 1906; ed. Univ. of Glasgow; junr. med. offr., Nig., 1933; med. offr., 1935; spec. gynaecology, 1949.

NANA-ATOO, C. H. O.—b. 1904; ed. Bremen Mission Sch., Santrokofi, Govt. Boys' Sch., Accra; 2nd div. clk., G.C., 1924; 1st div. clk., 1940; wireless stn. traffic supt., 1946; senr. radio offr., 1955 (Ghana civil service).

PANTON, Charles Stewart, C.P.M. —b. 1903; ed. elem. sch.; 2nd cl. constab., J'ca., 1923; 1st cl., 1927; cpl., 1931; sergt., 1933; sergt. maj., 1941; staff sergt. (later inspr.), 1947; sub. inspr. (later asst. supt.), 1948.

PARHAM, B. E. V., O.B.E.—b. 1902; ed. Christchurch Boys' High Sch. and Canterbury Univ. Coll., N.Z.; asst. agric. offr., Fiji, 1933; agric. offr., 1938; senr., 1946; dep. dir., agric., 1953; chmn., Fiji copra bd., rhinoceros beetle eradication bd., land conserv. bd., agric. advsy. coun., 1954; mem., research coun., S. Pac. comsn., 1949–54; author Fijian Plant Names , 1942; Alien Flora of Fiji , and Fijian Fungi , 1941; Naturalized Flora of Fiji with special ref. to Grasses, Legumes and Weeds ; Co-operative Societies in Fiji .

PHILLIPS, Sydney Thomas.—b. 1901; ed. St. Paul's Sch., Royal Horticultural Socy. Gdns., Wisley, and Royal Botanic Gdns., Kew; inspr. of plants and produce, G.C., 1925; senr. inspr., 1937; ch. inspr., 1947.
RITCHIE, W.—b. 1916; ed. Inverness Central Sch. and Higher Grade Sch.; stock verifier, Tang. rly., 1928; stores acctnt., G.C. rly., 1931; asst. stores supt., 1948.

SANDERSON, N., M.B.E. (1961).—b. 1910; ed. Richard Hind Cent. Sch., Stockton-on-Tees, and N.E. Sch. of Wireless, Newcastle-on-Tyne; wireless offr., E.A.P. & T. dept., 1939; gr. II, 1946; radio supt., 1947; serv. and sales supt., 1956; chief serv. and sales supt., 1957; telephone sales manager, 1960-61.
SHEARES, Benjamin Henry, L.M.S. (S'pore.), M.R.C.O.G. (Lond.).—b. 1907; ed. King Edward VII Coll. of Med., S'pore.; asst. med. offr., S'pore., 1929; gr. II, 1937; gr. I, 1937; med. offr., 1939.

SOUTAR, Easton Gerard. —b. 1901 ; ed. privately ; temp. asst., cts., J'ca., 1920 ; asst., 1922 ; dep. clk., leg. coun., 1924 ; 1st cl. clk., agric. loan soc. bd., 1938 ; senr. clk., 1944 ; examiner of accts., 1949.

SOUTAR, Arthur Dudley. —b. 1896 ; ed. Munro Coll. ; cl. asst., educ. dept., J'ca., 1915 ; asst. 1917 ; 2nd cl. clk., 1920 ; 1st cl., 1932 ; prin., 1942 ; asst. sec., 1944 ; sec. to various comtees., comsns. and a concil. bd., 1928–38.

STOCK, Frank.—b. 1902; ed. Oldham Sec. Sch. and Manchester High Sch. of Comm.; postmstr., B. Sol. Is. Prot., 1927; posts and tels. dept., Fiji, 1941; dist. admin., 1945; J.P., 1945; comsnr., sup. ct., 1945; acctnt., posts and tels., G.C., 1947.

STEENSON, Kingsley Rupert, M.B., Ch.B. (N.Z.).—b. 1895; ed. Waitaki Boys' High Sch. and Otago Univ. Med. Sch., N.Z.; on war service 1916–18, 1943–44, surg. lieut.-comdr., F.R.N.V.R.; Rockefeller Foundation. B. Sol. Is. Prot., 1927; med. offr., B. Sol. Is. Prot., 1929; med. offr., G. and E. Is. Col., 1931; S.M.O., 1940; med. offr., Fiji, 1943; S.M.O., 1948; D.D.M.S., 1948.

SUTTON, Stafford William Powell Foster, C.M.G. (1948), O.B.E. (Mil.) (1945), K.C. (1938).—b. 1897; ed. privately, St. Mary Magdalene Sch., Sussex, and Hon. Soc. of Gray's Inn; barrister-at-law; on mil. serv., 1914–26 and 1941–44, col.; solr.-gen., J'ca., 1936; ag. atty.-gen., Oct., 1936–Feb., 1937 and again from Nov., 1937–Feb., 1939; atty.-gen., Cyp., 1940; atty.-gen. and mem. for law and order, Ken., 1944; ag. gov., Ken., Aug. and Sept., 1947; dir. of man and woman power, Ken., 1944–45; chmn., lab. advsy. bd. and European civ. serv. advsy. bd., Ken.

TING, Siew Choon.—b. 1903; ed. Anglo-Chinese Schs., Kampar, Ipoh and S'pore., and Hong Kong Univ., B.A., teach. dip.; student teach., S'pore., 1920; S.S. gov. schol., H.K. Univ., 1920; asst. mstr., cl. I, S'pore., 1924; instr. in chem., 1935; superscale B, 1947; educ. offr., 1947; hdmstr., 1949.
VERBI, Albert Basil.—b. 1900; ed. Queen Elizabeth Gram. Sch., Blackburn; on war serv., 1918–20–24 and 1939–45, maj.; survr., T.T., 1935.

WATNEY, Henry Bryan.—b. 1901; ed. Oundle Sch. and Cambridge, B.A. (Cantab.); cadet, Uga., 1923; asst. dist. offr., 1926; dist. offr., 1936; senr. dist. offr., 1945; dist. comsnr., Lira (prov. admin.).

WHITE, Edwin George, F.R.C.V.S., B.Sc. (vet. sci.), B.Sc. (physiol.), Ph.D. (vet. sci.).—b. 1911 ; ed. Oxford and Newport (Mon.) High Schs, Royal Vet. Coll., London, and Univ. Coll., London ; Kitchener Memorial Schol., 1928–32 ; studentship for res. in animal health, 1932–35 ; lect. in path., res. inst. in animal path., Royal vet. coll., Lond., 1935 ; reader in path., 1939 ; p.s.o. Rowett res. inst., 1946 ; dir., E.A. vet. res. organ., 1947 ; visited Germany and Austria to report on vet. res. for min. agric., 1945 ; int. and ext. examr. for univ. of London bd. of vet. sci., and Royal coll. of vet. surg. ; author of some thirty publns. in sci. press on vet. sci. since 1934.

YEO, Bok Choon, M.B.E. (1947), L.M.S. (S'pore).—b. 1911 ; ed. King Edward VII Coll. of Med., S'pore. ; hse. surg., S'pore., 1937 ; med. offr., gr. II, 1940 ; senr. surg. tutor, 1941 ; med. offr., gr. II, 1945 ; dep. med. offr. and tutor, 1947 ; tutor in surg., faculty of med., Univ. of Mal., 1950.

CKWELL, HENRY FLEMING, B.A. Cantab— 884; ed. at Charterhouse (jun. and sen. arships), and at King's Coll., Camb., r.; asst. mast., Portsmouth gram. schl., 8; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1908; dist. offr., Jan., 1914; dist. offr., 2nd cls., , 1917; res., July, 1925.

MERY, WILLIAM BANKES, C.B.E. (1920).— 883; ed. Christs Hosp.; ent. cust. and excise st., 1901; asst. sec. (temp.) war trade dept., 6; estab. offr., ministry of transport, 1919; ance offr. (with rank of prin.) overseas settle- nt dept., D.O., 1922; rep. of the U.K. govt. the C'wealth of Australia under the Empire ttlement Act, 1925; prin., O.S.D., 1929; temp. d., D.O., 1932.

YES, HAROLD NEWTON, M.I.R.S.E.—B. ; signal inspr., Burma, May, 1907-June, ; rly. and signal engr., Australia, Apr., -Sept., 1913; signal and tel. inspr., constrn. , F.M.S. Rlys., Sept., 1913; asst. signal r, F.M.S. Rlys., 1917; district signal engr., e II, Jan. 1922; grade I, Jan., 1926; dist. l engr., Tampin, Apr., 1926; dist. signal tel. engr., Gemas, Aug., 1928.

AYNES, THOMAS EDWIN PERCIVAL, O.B.E. 2).—B. 1884; ed. St. Kitts Grammar l. and Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad; cal asst., col. sec.'s off., Trinidad, 1903; 6th C.S.O., 1903; 5th clk., C.S.O., 1906; 1st clk., office, Antigua, 1907; 3rd clk., C.S.O., 1911; clk. to admstr., Dominica, 1912; 2nd clk., O., clk. of exec. and legis. couns., Antigua, supt. of govt. printing office, 1912; clk. to str., and clk. to exec. and legis. couns., minica, 1919; offr. in ch. (later, warden), N. dist., 1924; temp. mem., leg. coun., Dominica, Feb., 1926; O.A.G., Montserrat, in Mar., 1926 and Apr., 1928; O.A.G., Virgin Is., Aug. to Nov., 1926; offl. mem., leg. coun., Dominica in 1927 and 1928; ag. asst. col. sec., Leeward Is., Apr.-Sept., 1927; treas., Dominica, June, 1928; offl. mem., exec. coun., Dominica, June, 1928; O.A.G., Montserrat, June-Sept., 1929; O.A.G., Dominica on three occasions in 1929, 1930 and 1931; comsnr., Montserrat, 1932.
IES, TUDOR THOMAS.—B. 1887; asst. rinter, Nyassaland Prot., 1916; ag. govt. and compt. stationery, May to July, 1917, 919 to Mar., 1920 and Apr. to Dec., 1923; inter., Dec., 1923.

THBERT, MORTON.—Ed. Edin. Institn. and W and West of Scot. Tech. Coll.; apprent. to Messrs. Dick, Kerr and Co., Ltd., Lon- 895; electrn. with Glasgow corptn. elec. 1899; asst. engnr. Govan corptn. elec. dept., chief asst. engnr., Oban corptn. elec. dept., electrl. engnr., B. Honduras, 1905; exec. electrl. engnr., P.W.D., B. Honduras, 1909; B.H.T.F., 1917; ag. adjutant, B.H.T.F., 1917 to Jan., 1918; hon. A.D.C. to gov., B. ras, 1919.

MERON, SIR DONALD CHARLES, C.G.M.G. , K.C.M.G. (1926), K.B.E. (1922), C.M.G. —B. 1872; ed. at Rathmines School, Dublin; Br. Guiana civ. ser., 1890; 5th class clk., variat, 1891; 4th ditto, 1895; 3rd class and tch clk., 1895; 2nd class, 1899; priv. to ag. govr., 1896, 1897, 1898 and 1901; st. gov. sec. and clk. of councils, 1900-1901, pal clk., secretariat, 1901; priv. sec. to govr. Newfoundland (with permission of S. of S.) on leave, 1902; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, 1904; ag. col. sec. in 1904-5-6-7; temp. fd. to S. Nigeria as asst. sec., Jan., 1908; pal asst. sec., 1911; sec. to S. Nigeria liquor inquiry comtee., 1909; ag. prov. comsnr. 1910, 1911 and 1912; ag. col. sec., 27th Feb. 1912, and 5th Sept. to 3rd Oct., 1912; y gov. in 1921-23; cent. sec., Nigeria, 1st 1914; ch. sec. to govt., 1st Jan., 1921; ag. n 1921, 1923 and 1924; gov. and comdr.-in- Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1924; ditto, ia, 1931.

ECHIEL, PERCY HUBERT, C.M.G. (1923). — 875; B.A. Bombay, 1889 (æt. 14); B.A., on, 1894; major schlr., Trin. Coll., Camb., ; 4th wrangler and B.A., Camb., 1897; apptd., compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., 11th Oct., ; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1903; to crown agts. for the colonies, May, 1905; of Fair Wages Advisory Comtee., 1909-1920; to Colonial Economic Development Comtee., 20; sec. and mem., E. African Currency bd., (resigned secretaryship 1921); crown agt. he cols., Dec., 1920; dir. of colonial scholars, ; rep. of C.O. on exec. coun. of Br. Empire bn., 1921-25; chmn., Palestine currency bd., ; mem., Hong Kong currency comsn., 1931.

LDENHUYS, FRANS EDUARD.—B. 1889; , 1910, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, S.A.; ed in U.S.A. at Univs. of Cornell, Wisconsin and California; B.Sc., Cornell , 1913 and Ph.D., 1917; teacher of nature and agrl. science, Grey Coll. Schl., 1918; "Die Landbou-weekblad," Bloemfontein, 1919; first ch., divn. of agrl. economics marketing, dept. of agr., S.A., 1925; under- or agr., 1926; dir., forestry, 1931.

CAEME, ALEXANDER WILLIAM SUTHERLAND, Mech.E.—B. 1882; ed. Malvern, pupil, 1902-6; held various appts. on Eng. Scottish rlys.; dep. loco. supdt., F.M.S. 1915-24; ag. loco. supt., F.M.S. rlys., 1917, Apr., 1919, Mar., 1922, Dec., 1923; supt., F.M.S. rlys., Mar., 1924; ch. mech. r., F.M.S. rlys., Oct., 1932.

EN, GEORGE. — B. 1894; ed. Westminster Schl.; on mil. serv., Oct., 1914 to Mar., 2nd lieut., The Buffs; wounded in France; clk., C.O., Oct., 1919 to Feb., 1920; miny. , Sept., 1920 to Dec., 1920; C.O., Dec., June, 1922; apptd. cler. offr., C.O., 21st 1922; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st June, 1928. EN, GEORGE C. — B. 1882; apptd., after exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. assigned to C.O., 26th Aug., 1901; cler. higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922.

OFFIN, NEVILL ALFRED MALCOLM.—B. ed. Blundell's; pol. probationer, S. ts., 1908; Feb., 1911; asst. supt. pol., Penang, 1912; asst. supt. pol., Malacca and asst. Chinese, July, 1912; seconded, Kedah, 1914; adjt., S. S. pol., Oct., 1916; supt. pol. ), Penang, Mar., 1923; C.P.O., Perak, 1931; dep. comsnr., pol., Selangor, 1932. FFITH, CARL.—Asst. auditor, Gold Coast, 1920.

RAHAM, RT. HON. GEORGE PERRY, P.C. (5), LL.D.—B. 1859; ed. High schls., Iroquois Morrisburg, Ontario; journalist; Morrisburg old, Ottawa Free Press, Brockville Recorder; ed to Ontario legislature for Brockville, 1898, and 1905; provl. sec. of Ontario, 1904; with the govt., 1905; leader of the opposition the Ontario legislature, 1907; sworn of the y coun. for Canada and min. of rlways. and ls, 1907; elected to the H. of C. for Brock-, by acclamation, 1907; defeated at g.e., ; re-elec. for S. Renfrew, bye-elec., 1912; did eek re-election at gen.-elec., 1917; re-el. g.e., , 1921; min. of militia and defence, and of l service in King admstn., Dec., 1921; min. ys. and canals, 1922; senator, Dec., 1926.

FFITH, SIR WM. BRANDFORD, KT. BACH. —B. 1858; Gilchrist scholar, 1877; B.A. , 1880; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., June, ag. Queen's advoc., G. Coast Col., Apr. to 84; dist. comsnr., 1885; ag. puisne judge, Jan., and Cape Coast, May, 1885; ag. ; advoc., 1886 and 1887; compiled ordi- of G. Coast Col., 1887; res. mag., Jamaica, L.M., St. Catherine, Feb., 1893; ag. atty.-ay to Dec., 1893; ch. just., G. Coast Col., 895; admstd. govt. of Lagos, July to 396; dep. for gov. G. Coast, Aug. to Nov., evised ordinances of G. Coast Col., 1898 3; retired, 1911.

ODDARD, THOMAS NELSON, M.B.E. (1918). — 889; ed. Wadham Coll., Oxford; B.A.; priv. sec. to gov., Falkland Is. and clk. of and legis. couns., Apr., 1914; lieut., Falk- Is. volr. force, Aug., 1914; capt., 1917; O.C., ; postal censor, 1914; proper offr. of the n, prize ct., 1914; chief clk., col. sec's office, , 1915; ag. comsnr. of currency, 1918; ag. audr., 1920; asst. col. sec., Sierra Leone, ; clk., exec. coun., 1921; A.D.C. to ag. gov., and to gov., 1922-23; census offr., 1921; ag. asst. col. sec. for various periods between and 1930; ag. col. sec. for various periods -33; ch. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1930; gov.'s dep. on various occasions, 1930 and 1931; compiler, Sierra Leone, Handbook 1924.

ES, JOHN WHITEHOUSE WARD.— ed. Oakham and Emmanuel Coll., s; B.A., 1905; M.A., 1919; cadet, 906; mag., Sept., 1911; offr., cls. II, 3; 1st mag., K. Lumpur, Apr., 1926; IB, comsnr., lands, F.M.S., continues ove July, 1928; ag. Br. res., Pahang, ); comsnr., lands, F.M.S., May, 1931; ., Negri Sembilan, July, 1931; Br. res., ibilan, Mar., 1932.

HRYS, H. L.—Ed. at gram. schl., senior Camb. certif, 3rd div. honours; easy., Antigua, Jan., 1892; 2nd indoor ; ag. 1st indoor offr. on several occa- ch. audit clk., Leeward Is., Dec., 1897, 1898; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, 3, to Mar., 1904; 1st indoor offr. of ntigua, Apr., 1904; ag. ch. audit clk. al acctnt., Leeward Is., May to Oct., 1909, Aug., 1910, and from Apr., 1911 to July, 1912; ag. treasr. of Antigua, and federal treasr. from Dec., 1912 to Apr., 1913.

ARHAM, ARTHUR DOUGLAS, F.R.I.B.A., I., Chartered Archt. and Quantity Surv., n., Town Planning Inst.—B. 1890 ; ed. King's L., London ; temp. architectural asst., P.W.D., Lon., 1915 ; 1st asst. archt., 1918 ; ag. ch. archt. 919, 1923, 1925, 1927 and 1929 ; attd., town planning divn., Miny. of Health, 1926.
TITCOMBE, FRED. — B. 1885; European stn. mast., F.M.S. Rlys., Dec., 1910; shorthand writer and typist, Jan., 1912; asst. sec. to gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., Mar., 1924.

ARKER, CECIL JOSEPH, M.C., M.I.C.E.— 1888 ; asst. engnr., Peru, 1910 ; asst. engnr., V.D., F.M.S., Jan., 1911 ; lieut., R.E. old Co.), 1915-19 ; served in France and Italy ; it. in despa., 1917 ; M.C., 1918 ; war and ory meds. ; ex. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Jan., 0 ; sen. ex. engnr., Apr., 1927 ; state engnr., ak, Jan., 1930.
LAND, JOHN STOCKWIN, M.B.E., B.A., M.I.A.—Draughtsman, P.W.D., tal, Mar., 1909; asst. archt., P.W.D., of S. Africa, Apr., 1912; archt., Sept., ec. for pub. wks., June, 1932.

ARKINSON, A. C. C., M.A. (1910), C.M.G. 31), O.B.E.—B. 1884 ; ed. at Epsom and ford : class. demy, Magdalen. 1903-7 ; 1st cls. da., 1905 ; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1907 ; apptd. after apet. exam., 1st cls. clk., acctnt.-gen.'s dept., miralty, Feb., 1908 ; transfd. as 2nd cls. clk., D., 19th Apr., 1909 ; sec., man. comtee. of perial bureau of entomology ; sec. to comtee. std. to investigate relationship between wild mals and trypanosomiasis, 1913-1914 ; asst. v. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, 5th Oct., 1914, to Bonar Law, 27th May, 1915, to Mr. Walter ng, 11th Dec., 1916 ; 1st cls. clk., 8th Dec., 17 ; on military service from 16th May, 1915 to 26th May, 1919 ; prin., 1st Apr., 1920 ; priv. sec. to Lord Milner, 1st Oct., 1920 ; asst. sec., 1st Oct., 1925 ; order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, cls. III, 1929 ; asst. under sec. of state, 15th Aug., 1931.

CCLES, LAUNCELOT WILLIAM GREGORY, —B. 1890; ed. Michaelhouse Sch., Natal and cester Coll., Oxford; asst. survr., N. Rho- a, Oct., 1912; on war serv., 1914-20; asst. r., diagrams, Apr., 1921; ag. ch. survr. from , 1922; dep. dir., surveys and examr., dia- ns and surveys, Oct., 1924; ag. dir., surveys 925, 1928 and 1931; comsnr., lands, mines local govt. and M.L.C., 1933 and 1936.
INTER, K. L.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. a Prot., Apr., 1919.

LOUD, SIR FRANCIS LEWIS CASTLE, K.C.B. (31), C.B. (1918)—B. 1875; ed. Cranleigh Schl. King's Coll., London; barrister-at-law (Lincoln's Inn); ent. bd. of agr., 1894; pvte. sec. to Thomas Elliott (1904-5), Mr. Ailwyn Fellowes (rd Ailwyn) (1905) and Lord Carrington (Marquis of Lincolnshire) (1906-7) as presdts. of bd. agr.; head, land branch, 1907-14; asst. sec., 4-19; permtd. sec., miny. of agr. and fisheries, 1920; chmn., bd. of cust. and excise, 1927; mt. sec., min. of lab., 1930; high comsnr. in Canada for H.M.'s govt. in the U.K., 1934.

CCLES, WILLIAM HUNTER.—Ed. St. Paul's l., London; clk., educn. office, Trinidad, 1st ., 1907; clk. to comsns. of currency, e, 1908; customs, Jan., 1911; temp. sub- r. of pol., Nov., 1914 to Feb., 1915; on mily. serv., it, afterwards capt., 1915-19; returned to cust.. nidad, July, 1919; on sp. mil. duty to B. iana and Trinidad, Aug. to Oct.; asst. recr.. Gambia, Oct., 1919; relinquished comsn., h rank of capt., Sept., 1921; A.D.C. and v. sec. to Mr. G. Gwyn, Sept., 1921 and to . C. R. M. Workman, Sept., 1921 to Jan., 92; ag. clk., exec. coun., Sept., 1921 to Jan.. 92; offr. in-charge, posts and tels. dept., Feb. July, 1924; offr. in-charge, cust. on various asions between 1923 and 1928; asst. comptr., st., Sierra Leone, 1928; ag. comptr., cust., various occasions, 1929-32; comptr., cust., 32.

OYDELL, HON. THOMAS.—Served as engnr. at E & W. Hawthorn; B. 1887; ent. Peters, nr. Newcastle; warden, county Africa, 1903; worked at D. counties, Apr., 1936. br. wks. and al-JOR-GEN., ANDRIES JACOB rban; stood up, D.S.O.—Joined civ. serv., S. African Labour 1896; served Mpfu (native) cam- rban at the Zoutpansberg and Anglo-Boer War, tested by-elected veldt cornet, S. African Repub. 1895-20, 21 and apt., permanent force (staff), Union labour Party, 1912; promoted major, and serveducus; natl. offr., No. 6 and later as dist. staff d tel. a 7 mil. dist., 1914; brig.-major, 1st line, 1924; ag., German S.W.A. campaign, 1915; nator, S. ut.-col., Mar., 1915; dist. staff offr., BOYEal. dist., with rank of temp. lieut-col., lbert 20 emplt., Aug., 1915; brev. lieut.-col., pt., 1915; brig.-major, 2nd mounted brig., E. 1919; 1, Mar., 1916; gen. staff offr. with rank of 1936. leadqrs., 3rd E. African divn., Apr., 1916; BCmed duty as dist. staff offr. No. 6 mil. 1922. Dec., 1916; transf. to defence headqrs., and d. umed duty as asst. adjt.-gen., defence headqrs., pt., 1917; ag. ch. of gen. staff and adjt.-gen., 5, 1th temp. rank of col., Nov., 1919; lieut.-col., 1st grade staff offr., Nov., 1919; brig.-gen., and ch. of gen. staff, Sept., 1920; combined post of ch. of gen. staff and sec. for defence, 1st Oct., 1922; maj.-gen., Nov., 1929; sec. for defence and G.O.C., Union Defence Forces, May, 1933; 1914-15 Star, Br. War Med., Victory Med., D.S.O., (1917), French Croix de Guerre; men. in desps., for serv. in G.S.W.A. campaign (1918), medal, Anglo-Boer oorlog.

IGHTBOURN, J. L.—B. 1889; cadet, Out ands, Bahamas, 1910; ag. 3rd and 2nd clk., col. 's office, 1910-12; sec., bd. of educn., Mar., 1914; , pol. comsn., 1921-22; ag. audr., pub. accts., y-Nov., 1924 and from Jan. to Apr., 1926; ag. asnr., Out Islands, Nov., 1926 to May, 1927; , grade I., audit dept., July, 1927; ag. audr., various occasions, 1929-36. J. KING HONG, B.A. (Hong Kong)—B. 1891; edmr., vernacular middle schl., Hong Kong, 1926.
ETERS, BERNARD R., M.B.E., A.M.I.C.E.— st. engr., E.A.P., 1914-15; exec. engr., nganyika, 1919; ag. dep. D.P.W. in 1926 and 27; D.P.W., Nyassaland, Aug., 1928.

GRANNIS, O.B.E.— steel bounties, Sydney, d commerce, Canada, 1902; Apr., 1911; on mily. serv., (ont. in desps.); head clk., Apr., Jan., 1921; asst. dep. min., T. and 23; on sp. duty to U.K., 1928; dep. min. of commerce, Nov., 1931.

packets.reason: year_start T. and 23 not in source

source_text: ORTON, HENRY JOHN STEPHEN, M.B.E. (4).—B. 1896; cler. asst., Gibraltar, 1913; ne and wharf clk., 1914; treas. clk., 1914; sed compet. exam., 1917; clk.-in-charge, impts. l expts., and clk. to food contr., 1914 to 1920; . asst. shipping master, 1921; asst. shipping ster, 1922; sec. and treas., sailors' home, 1920: -in-charge port dept. and ag. Trinity agt., in 24-26; asst. sec., col. sec's. office, 1927; pvte. . and interpreter to gov., 1929.

TOODLEY, EDWIN ALAN.—B. 1890; ed. unleigh Schl.; col. audit dept., Mar., 1915; t. audr., Nigeria, May, 1915 (seconded to E. rica, mily. audit, 1916-19, asst. audr., maliland, 1919); dep. audr., Gold Coast, July, 33; ag. audr., May, 1934.
ILSON, PERCIVAL DOUGLAS, M.Inst.C.E.— 1887; ed. Swansea Gram. Schl.; asst. nr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1913; exec. engnr., 4.

ONEHAVEN, 1ST BARON (cr. 1925), RT. SIR JOHN LAWRENCE BAIRD, BART., P.C. ), G.C.M.G. (1925), C.M.G. 1904, D.S.O. ), J.P., D.L.—B. 1874; ed. Eton and Christ ch, Oxford; ent. diplomatic service, Vienna, ; Cairo, 1898; Abyssinia, 1899; pvte. sec. to U. Garstin, under-sec., pub. wks., Egypt, 02; ag. agt. and cons. gen., Abyssinia, 1902; t. offr., Br. E. Africa and Abyssinia frontier rev, 1902-3; polit. offr., Abyssinian army in naliland, 1903-4 (med. and clasp, despa., I.G.); 2nd sec., Paris, 1904-6; Buenos Aires, 6-8; served as intelligence offr., European , 1914-15 (desps., D.S.O.); M.P., Rugby, 0-22; Ayr Burghs, 1922-25; parly. mem., Air . 1916-18; parly. under sec., R.A.F., 1918; ly. under sec., H.O., 1919-22; min. of trans- t, 1922-24; 1st comsnr. of wks., 1924; gov. t. and commdr.-in-ch., Commonwealth of Aus- lia, 1925-31.

ILSON, BRIG.-GEN. SIR SAMUEL HERBERT, C.M.G. (1929), K.C.B., (1927), K.C.M.G. (1923), B.E. (1921), C.B. (1918), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 73; entd. Army, R.E., 1893; capt., 1904; major 13; bt. lieut.-col., 1916; bt. col., 1917; served in Africa, 1899-1900 (Queen's medal, two clasps); European War, 1914-18; Chevalier Legion d'Honneur, 1915; Commandeur de la Couronne Belge, 1918; Officier Legion d'Honneur, 1918; ench croix de guerre avec palme; Belgian croix guerre; sec. to overseas defence comtee., 1911-; Gen. staff offr., 2nd grade, 1914; gen. staff ir., 1st grade, 1915; brig.-gen. gen. staff, 1916; sec. to overseas defence comtee., 19th Nov., 1918; v., Trinidad, 7th Nov., 1921; gov., Jamaica, Sept., 1924; assumed govt., 29th Sept., 1924; permt. under sec. of state for the cols., 6th Aug., 1925; ret., 1933.

BONEILL, T. H. M., B.Sc., M.I.Mech.E., Whitworth scholar.—Ed. at Shrewsbury and Swindon Tech. Coll.; articed to rly. engineering at Swindon (G.W.R.) works; engaged as junr. asst.-engnr. on construction, Severn tunnel; asst.-engnr. on L.S. and M.S. Rly., U.S.A.; asst. to divisional loco. engnr., C.P.R.; engnr. in charge of G.W.R. West London wks. (loco., carriage and wagon dept.); loco. carriage and wagon supt., Jamaica Govt. rly.; engnr. and asst. gen. man., Cent. American rlys.; loco. and carriage supt., Lagos govt. rly., Mar. 1903; ag. dep. gen. man., 2nd Apr. to 17th June, 1907; and from Sept., 1908, to Feb., 1909; capt., Lagos rly. volunteers.

LOVELACE, LOVELACE HARTE.—B. 1858; clk. of the peace, Trinidad, 1876; clk. of cust., 1877; audit offr., 1878; prin. clk., sup. ct., 1887; also comsnr. of oaths; ag. dep. registr., 1896-7-8, 1900, 1903, 1906 and 1907; dep. registr., 12th Dec., 1910.
BRENNAN, G.—Late 1st W. I. Regiment; assistant inspector, Gold Coast Constabulary, 22nd Nov., 1880; employed on mission to the Prah, June, 1884; acted as inspector-general in 1886.

flagged_value.position: employed on mission to the Prah

source_text: CHADWICK, F. M.—Chief clerk at the royal gaol, Trinidad, Nov. 1, 1859; sub-inspector of police, Trinidad, Jan. 1, 1861; and superintendent of military police; and immigration agent at St. Lucia, June 8, 1864; also inspector of prisons; on the cessation of immigration to the colony was, in July, 1869, appointed chief revenue officer in conjunction with his duties as superintendent of police; and inspector of revenue and police, January 1, 1872; treasurer of Grenada, October, 1872.
GIBBS, J. HATCH.—Barrister-at-law, controller of stamps, Victoria, Dec., 1879.

IBBOTT, JOHN THOMAS.—Sub-controller of customs and sub-bookkeeper, Berbice, 16th March, 1875; acting tide waiter, Georgetown, Demerara, from the 9th July, 1869, to the 1st of Feb., 1860; clerk in charge of the colonial bonded warehouse, Georgetown, 1st Feb., 1860; acting sub-controller of customs and sub-bookkeeper, Berbice, from the 9th Sept., 1869, to the 23rd December, 1870; acting assistant government secretary, and acting receiver-general, Berbice, from the 24th Dec., 1870, to the 23rd March, 1872; appointed on the 16th Feb., 1870, 1st clerk to the customs, Georgetown, Demerara; assumed the duties on the 23rd March, 1872; acted as comptroller of customs in 1872, 1874, and 1876.

INGLOTT, FERDINAND VINCENT, C.M.G. (1868).—Educated in a training college in England at public expense, under the personal direction of the late Sir James P. Kay Shuttleworth, Bart., and of E. C. Tufnell, Esq., inspector of schools, to be a "model teacher" of the new system of primary education; teacher of the new system, and director of the Malta government primary schools reformed according to that system, 1839-48; on the suspension of the educational reform, appointed to assist the secretary and chief clerk in the admiral-superintendent's office, and promoted to other situations in the naval department, 1843-55; assistant superintendent of ports to carry out the provisions of the Merchant Shipping Act, 1854-55-58; comptroller of charitable institutions, and ex officio member of council, Jan., 1859-70; executive commissioner for Malta at the London International Exhibition of 1862, and local commissioner for the Paris Exhibition of 1867; collector of customs, and ex officio member of the executive and legislative councils, Feb., 1870-85; postmaster-general, Jan., 1886.
